steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim says Mrs. Sommers "becomes engrossed in the decision" of how to spend the money. The correct quotation must show her mentally absorbed in deliberating about that decision — not shopping, not reacting to the money itself.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "The question of investment was one that occupied her greatly… absorbed in speculation and calculation": shows her mind fully consumed by deliberating how to spend the money. ✓
  • Choice B — "she had learned to clutch a piece of goods and hold it…": describes her shopping behavior in a store, not her deliberation about how to spend. ✗
  • Choice C — "A young girl… asked her if she wished to examine their line of silk hosiery": a shopping scene; shows what she does after the decision, not the decision itself. ✗
  • Choice D — "It seemed to her a very large amount of money… a feeling of importance": shows her emotional reaction to having the money, not engrossment in deciding how to spend it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
